summary: A biographical overview of Judea Pearl, detailing his foundational contributions to artificial intelligence, Bayesian networks, and causal inference. While thin on his scientific methodologies, the article highlights his personal philosophies, including his response to the tragic murder of his son Daniel Pearl, and his self-described identity as a 'practicing disbeliever' who maintains Jewish traditions without a belief in God.

principles.rationale: In response to the murder of his son by terrorists, he resolved to work towards reconciliation between Jews and Muslims rather than succumbing to hatred.
principles.source_quote: Hate killed my son. Therefore I am determined to fight hate.

principles.statement: Embrace cultural and religious traditions regardless of literal belief.
principles.rationale: He identifies as a 'practicing disbeliever', maintaining rituals like laying tefillin and hosting Shabbat dinners because they connect him to his heritage, even though he is a strict atheist.
principles.source_quote: No, I don't believe in a God [that] would listen to me. But I do pray every morning.

mental_models.name: Store of Metaphors
mental_models.description: Relying on the cultural and historical narratives of one's upbringing as a lens to interpret the world and communicate, independent of literal religious belief.
mental_models.example: Drawing on the Bible and Jewish history to form his worldview and metaphors, while firmly stating he knows there is no God.

signature_quotes.text: Hate killed my son. Therefore I am determined to fight hate.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining to Rabbi Jonathan Sacks why he works for reconciliation between Jews and Muslims.

signature_quotes.text: I turned secular at the age of 11, by divine revelation. [Laughs.] I was standing on the roof of the house my father built, looking down on the street and suddenly it became very clear to me that there is no God.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing his religious views and loss of faith.

signature_quotes.text: I'm, of course, prisoner of my upbringing, which means my store of metaphors comes from the Bible and comes from history of the Jewish people. But I don't believe in God. Actually, I know there isn't [a] God.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining his connection to Jewish tradition despite being an atheist.
